channel.title: Mahdi Hazrati Tech Blog
channel.description: Notes on React, Next.js, design systems, and shipping products.

item.title: The Day the Facility Guy Shipped a Feature to My App
item.description: I taught our building's maintenance guy how to vibe code for fun. Twenty minutes later he'd chatted his way into a working feature inside one of my real apps. Here's what actually happened, and why it didn't disprove anything I've written in this series.

item.title: Are You Orchestrating or Vibing? A Maturity Model for AI-Assisted Engineering
item.description: A five-level, self-scoring checklist to find out honestly whether your AI-assisted workflow is AI-Orchestrated Engineering or vibe coding with extra steps.

item.title: Orchestration Patterns: Directing AI Like a Systems Architect
item.description: Pipeline, fan-out/fan-in, adversarial verification, judge panels the distributed-systems patterns that make AI-Orchestrated Engineering practical instead of aspirational.

item.title: The Five Layers of AI-Orchestrated Engineering
item.description: Intent, Decomposition, Delegation, Verification, Synthesis the framework I use to turn 'AI writes code fast' into 'AI-assisted systems are correct.'

item.title: The Vibe Coding Trap: Why 'Just Let the AI Do It' Fails at Scale
item.description: Vibe coding feels fast because it defers all its cost to later. A field guide to the five failure modes I see over and over and why they're structural, not incidental.

item.title: AI-Orchestrated Engineering: A New Discipline Beyond Vibe Coding
item.description: Vibe coding treats AI as an oracle you gamble on. AI-Orchestrated Engineering treats it as an instrument you conduct. Here's the discipline I've been building and why the distinction matters.

item.title: Every Markdown Feature This Blog Supports
item.description: A living reference for writing posts thumbnails, code, tables, alerts, footnotes, images, audio, video, and YouTube embeds.

item.title: Perlin Noise: The Algorithm Behind Procedural Worlds
item.description: How Ken Perlin's gradient noise creates infinite terrain, clouds, and fire and why Simplex improved it thirty years later.

item.title: Transformers Explained: Attention, QKV, and Why LLMs Work
item.description: A frontend engineer's guide to the architecture behind GPT self-attention, positional encoding, and the encoder-decoder split.

item.title: Ray Marching and Signed Distance Fields
item.description: Render infinite geometry on a fullscreen triangle SDFs, sphere tracing, and the shader loop that replaced polygon soup.

item.title: Concurrent React: Suspense, Transitions, and Time Slicing
item.description: How React 18 schedules updates, keeps the UI responsive during heavy renders, and what useTransition actually buys you.

item.title: B-Trees: Why Every Database Index Looks the Same
item.description: The data structure behind PostgreSQL, SQLite, and MySQL indexes balanced trees, page alignment, and why O(log n) isn't enough.

item.title: File-Based Blogs on Next.js Static Export
item.description: How to ship a markdown blog with zero database, full SEO, and static export the same stack powering mahdi.is-a.dev.

item.title: WebAssembly Beyond the Browser
item.description: From Figma's design engine to Cloudflare Workers why WASM is becoming the portable bytecode layer of the web stack.

item.title: Raft Consensus: Leader Election Without the PhD
item.description: How distributed systems agree on a log when nodes fail terms, heartbeats, and why Paxos stayed in papers while Raft got textbooks.

item.title: Gradient Descent: The Hill-Climbing Algorithm Behind Deep Learning
item.description: Loss surfaces, learning rates, momentum, and Adam the optimization loop that makes neural networks actually learn.

item.title: OAuth2 + PKCE: Auth for Modern Single-Page Apps
item.description: Why implicit flow died, how PKCE protects public clients, and the token flow every React app should implement.

item.title: Git Internals: Objects, Refs, and the DAG Under the Hood
item.description: Git isn't a filesystem with history it's a content-addressed object database with labels. Once you see the DAG, everything clicks.

item.title: Design Systems That Actually Ship
item.description: Lessons from building NPUI tokens, primitives, and the gap between Figma and production.

item.title: Privacy-First Analytics Without the Tradeoffs
item.description: Building Analytick taught me that you can have real-time dashboards and respect user privacy if you design constraints in from day one.
